name: Porst 2012 Tadalafil ED Endothelial Measures Extract
population_or_context: PDE5-inhibitor-naive men with erectile dysfunction; mean age 52 years.
sample_size: 215 randomized: tadalafil once daily N=146; placebo N=69.
methods: Randomized placebo-controlled 12-week trial of tadalafil 5 mg once daily, with possible down-titration to 2.5 mg; endothelial endpoints included biomarkers and peripheral arterial tonometric measures.
population_profile: Men with ED, mean age 52; indirect for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 5 mg once daily with possible down-titration to 2.5 mg for 12 weeks.
indication_context: On-label erectile-dysfunction treatment context with exploratory endothelial measures.
study_design: Randomized placebo-controlled parallel trial in PDE5-inhibitor-naive ED patients.
safety_findings: Abstract reports tadalafil was well tolerated and lists back pain, headache, and dyspepsia as adverse events.
directness_to_target_population: Low-to-moderate for tadalafil exposure and common daily dose, low for healthy target population because participants had ED.
confounding_and_bias_limits: ED population, exploratory endothelial measures, abstract-level extraction, and no clinical cardiovascular outcomes.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; transfer limit is ED population, dose-duration context, and surrogate endpoint specificity.
